free_text: Proton induced thick target gamma-ray yields of light
nuclei at the energy region Ep=1.0 - 4.1 MeV.
Institute of Nuclear Technology, Radiation
protection, Institute of Nuclear Physics.
Department of Physics
The detector used was an intrinsic Germanium
gamma-ray detector, with a resolution of 1.9 keV at the
Eg=1333 keV gamma-ray, placed at angle 90.degr. with
respect to the beam
The target was cooled at temperature +10 C in
order to withstand high beam current.
The target used were pellets of powder graphite and
cellulose, mixed with less than 10% of a compound
containing the element under investigation in the
isotopic abundance found in nature.  Four pellet of
different concentration were prepared and analysed for
each elements.
Any analysis of experimental uncertainties is absent.
The yields given are corrected for the efficiency of
detector and reduced to yield of monoatomic target.
Possible overlap with g-ray from another reactions is
discussed.
Application of the data for the analysis of a clay
sample is presented.
Correction according to O.Schwerer remarks.
Last checking has been done.
SD:Illegal blanks deleted from author's list
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
The datum is given in separate data table.
